ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for a spicy tomato chicken burger feast set meal. FOODON_00002473 (chicken sandwich food product) is the closest defensible anchor. The feast format (with fries, drink, and pie) is not individually represented in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FHR (Chicken burger) is the closest defensible match. The full feast set (fries, soft drink, pie) is not captured by a single FoodEx2 code; the burger component is used as the primary anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for a composite fast-food feast set meal of this type.

serving.description: 1 feast set (burger + M fries + drink + pie)
serving.natural_language_portion: One typical feast set serving comprising a spicy tomato chicken burger, medium-sized French fries, a sugar-sweetened soft drink, and a pie, estimated at approximately 650 g total combined weight.

nutrient_source.title: Spicy Tomato Chicken Burger Feast — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values per 100 g are taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for the Spicy Tomato Chicken Burger Feast (cr_id G00010114). All per_100g macros and micronutrients are sourced from this canonical HPB record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; and University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for white-flour burger bun, French fries, and sugar-sweetened soft drink
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No single GI value exists for this composite feast set. GI of 72 is estimated as a weighted composite: white-flour burger bun GI ~71 (University of Sydney GI Database), deep-fried French fries GI ~75 (University of Sydney GI Database), sugar-sweetened cola GI ~63 (University of Sydney GI Database), and pie pastry GI ~59 (analogous estimate). Weighted by approximate carbohydrate contribution of each component across the feast. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI 72 × available carbohydrate per serving (approx. 192 g carbohydrate in 650 g serving at 29.57 g/100 g) / 100 = 138.5 GL units. This is a high-GL meal.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (487 mg/100 g) with low potassium offset (197 mg/100 g), compounded by salted fries, spicy tomato sauce, mayonnaise, and a sugar-sweetened soft drink across the full feast
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this feast set due to its high sodium load and the inclusion of a sugar-sweetened beverage; if consumed, replace the soft drink with water and request reduced sauce quantities to partially mitigate sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycaemic index meal (estimated GI 72) with 29.57 g carbohydrate per 100 g and only 1.8 g dietary fibre per 100 g; the feast adds a sugar-sweetened soft drink and deep-fried high-GI French fries, producing a very high glycaemic load of approximately 138 GL units per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this feast set; if consumed, the sugar-sweetened soft drink must be replaced with water or a diet beverage, and French fries should be substituted with a side salad to substantially reduce the glycaemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Ultra-processed deep-fried meal with trans fat (0.1 g/100 g), high sodium (487 mg/100 g), high cholesterol (29 mg/100 g), and a sugar-sweetened soft drink; the combination of trans fat, saturated fat from deep-frying, and high sodium is strongly atherogenic
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this feast set due to the presence of trans fat, high sodium, and deep-fried components; frequent consumption is associated with increased LDL cholesterol, arterial inflammation, and elevated cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried chicken patty and French fries generate advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); trans fat (0.1 g/100 g) is present; tomato sauce contains nightshade solanine; sugar-sweetened soft drink promotes inflammatory mediators
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this feast set entirely, particularly during flares, as the deep-fried components generate AGEs, trans fat is pro-inflammatory, and the sugar-sweetened beverage further promotes systemic inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Spicy Tomato Chicken Burger Feast is a Singapore fast-food set meal comprising a deep-fried spiced chicken patty in a refined-wheat-flour burger bun topped with spicy tomato sauce and mayonnaise, served alongside medium-sized deep-fried French fries, a sugar-sweetened soft drink, and a pie. The burger patty is seasoned with chilli and spices before deep-frying, producing a crispy exterior with a juicy interior. The spicy tomato sauce adds a tangy, mildly hot flavour profile. The feast format is a high-calorie, ultra-processed meal typical of Western-style fast-food chains operating in Singapore. Nutritionally, the meal provides 263 kcal per 100 g, with 13.3 g protein, 10.18 g fat, and 29.57 g carbohydrate per 100 g. The sodium content is high at 487 mg/100 g, and the meal contains 0.1 g trans fat per 100 g from deep-frying. The estimated glycaemic index of the full feast is approximately 72 (high), with a glycaemic load of approximately 138 GL units per serving — making it clinically unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The inclusion of a sugar-sweetened beverage is a key disqualifying factor for multiple chronic disease conditions. The meal is widely consumed as a lunch or dinner option in Singapore and is representative of the Western fast-food category in the local food environment.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values per 100 g are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry for this exact product (cr_id G00010114) with a stated confidence of 0.95. The source is an approved primary regional source. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component values, which reduces GI confidence to medium, but overall record confidence remains high given the exact nutrient match.
notes: saturated_fat_g is set to -1: detailed saturated fat breakdown for this composite feast product is not separately reported in the HPB Foodo database entry as of 2026-04-16. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions are not available in the HPB Foodo database for this product. water_g is set to -1: moisture content for this composite feast product is not reported in the HPB Foodo database. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are set to -1: micronutrient detail beyond the reported fields is not available in the HPB Foodo database entry for this product. trans_fat_g of 0.1 g/100 g is carried forward from the input health_suitability context as a clinically noted value; this is consistent with deep-fried fast-food products but is not explicitly listed as a separate field in the HPB source — flagged for source verification. GI of 72 is a weighted analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component values (burger bun, fries, soft drink, pie); no single composite GI value exists for this feast set. Serving size of 650 g is an estimate for the full feast set (burger + medium fries + soft drink + pie); actual weight may vary by outlet. Halal status is assumed based on the Singapore fast-food context and typical HPB-listed products; independent halal certification should be verified.
